title: Physical Integrity Rights Score
subtitle: The score captures the extent to which citizens are protected from government killings, torture, political imprisonments, extrajudicial executions, mass killings and disappearances. Large positive scores mean abuses are rare relative to other countries and years, large negative scores that abuses are relatively widespread.
note: The scores are based on a statistical model that combines measures from several other sources.
caption:
Physical Integrity Rights Score

The score captures the extent to which citizens are protected from government killings, torture, political imprisonments, extrajudicial executions, mass killings and disappearances. Large positive scores mean abuses are rare relative to other countries and years, large negative scores that abuses are relatively widespread.

This choropleth world map displays the Physical Integrity Rights Score for 2019, based on a statistical model that combines measures from several other sources. The legend ranges from -4 to +4, where large positive scores indicate rare abuses and large negative scores indicate widespread abuses. The darkest blue shades, representing the highest scores near +4, are visible in Canada, Norway, Sweden, Finland, Iceland, New Zealand, and Uruguay. Surrounding these nations, much of Western Europe, Australia, and parts of South America appear in lighter blue shades, generally falling between 0 and +3. In contrast, the darkest red and orange shades, indicating negative scores between -1 and -4, are concentrated in specific regions. Algeria, Sudan, Yemen, and Myanmar sit in the darkest red bands, suggesting scores near -3 or -4. Broad swathes of the Middle East, North Africa, and parts of Asia display mid-range orange shades, typically falling between -1 and -2. The United States and Mexico appear in light orange or near-zero shades. Large portions of central Africa and Greenland are marked with diagonal hatching to indicate no data. The visual distribution shows the highest scores clustered in Northern Europe, North America, and Oceania, while the lowest scores are scattered across parts of Africa, the Middle East, and Southeast Asia.
